description_long: This bungalow features a single-story rectangular structure with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade is clad in wood paneling and includes four large, dark-framed windows symmetrically arranged on either side of a central entrance. The entrance is sheltered by a projecting porch with a smaller gabled roof supported by two white posts, and a partially open door is visible. The sides and rear of the building are simpler, with fewer or no windows and a continuation of the wood and dark trim. The roof has a complex intersection of gables above the porch, adding architectural interest. The overall appearance is modern yet traditional, with clean lines and practical materials. This bungalow is suitable for residential neighborhoods and suburban settings.
description: A single-story wooden bungalow with a gray gable roof, symmetrical front windows, and a covered porch entrance. The design is modern-traditional with clean lines and practical materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical window arrangement, central entrance with a gabled porch, and partially open door.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the extended gable roof, porch structure, and minimal side windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain facade with no visible windows and continuous wood paneling.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the porch extension and the intersection of roof gables.
